subtitle: Subungual haematoma — very common in marathon runners

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Almost inevitable in marathon training — preventable with correct shoe fit

mild.description: Darkening under one or more toenails, mild pressure sensation
mild.can_run_note: Yes — it will resolve on its own over several weeks
why: Repeated toe impact against the front of the shoe during downhill running
Shoe too short or laced too loosely allowing the foot to slide forward
Blood pooling under the nail from repeated micro-trauma
relief: No treatment needed for mild cases — the nail will grow out
Ensure shoes are half a thumb-width longer than your longest toe
Lace shoes more firmly to stop foot sliding forward
Keep toenails trimmed short — straight across, not rounded
prevention: Buy running shoes 0.5–1 size larger than casual shoes
Trim toenails before every long run

moderate.description: Painful pressure under nail, nail lifting from nail bed
moderate.can_run_note: Run if tolerable — pad the nail with foam tape for protection
why: Significant subungual haematoma creating pressure under the nail
Nail bed separation from repeated impact
Possible nail infection if skin is broken
relief: Drain the haematoma: sterile needle heated red-hot, pierce the nail to release blood — immediate pressure relief
Cover with sterile dressing after draining
Anti-bacterial ointment if skin is broken around the nail
Protect with foam toe cap during runs
prevention: Correct shoe sizing — this is entirely preventable
If repeated nails affected — check foot measurement professionally

severe.description: Nail fully detached, nail bed exposed, infection signs
severe.can_run_note: Rest until nail bed has sufficient new nail cover
why: Complete nail avulsion exposing the sensitive nail bed
Risk of nail bed infection without protection
Possible nail bed damage affecting future nail regrowth
relief: Keep nail bed clean and covered with non-stick sterile dressing
See a GP if signs of infection: increasing redness, swelling, pus, fever
A new nail will grow over 3–6 months
prevention: Once the nail regrows, permanently adjust shoe sizing
Consider nail protection products for ultras and marathons

see_physio_if: Signs of infection around the nail: heat, swelling, pus, red streaking
Nail bed damage affecting nail regrowth after 6 months
